methods.description: Collection of sediment cores from the Ba Lai palaeochannel area and initial processing for laboratory analysis.

steps.description: Eight cores were collected from the area of Ba Lai palaeochannel in 2019.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment cores from Ba Lai palaeochannel

steps.description: The surface elevations of the core sites were estimated from topographic maps and varied from 0.9 to 2.0 m.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate surface elevations from topographic maps

methods.description: Constraining the margins of the palaeochannel using digital elevation models and satellite imagery.

steps.description: The margins of the palaeochannel were constrained in QGIS using a Digital Elevation Model (DEM) from the Shuttle Radar Topography Mission (SRTM) obtained from the United States Geological Survey and satellite images from the Google Plugin.
steps.searchable_summary: use QGIS with DEM and satellite images to constrain palaeochannel margins

methods.description: Laboratory analysis of cores including splitting, photographing, description, and sampling for various tests.

steps.description: In the laboratory, cores were split, photographed, described and sampled for X-radiography, shell identification and age dating.
steps.searchable_summary: split, photograph, describe, and sample cores for analysis

steps.description: The description included the recording of the sediment grain size, vertical trends, sedimentary structures, as well as trace and body fossils, and was used, together with the general context of the palaeochannel, to differentiate nine sedimentary facies.
steps.searchable_summary: record and analyze sediment characteristics to differentiate facies

steps.description: Bioturbation intensity was also recorded using the Bioturbation Index (BI) of Taylor & Goldring (1993).
steps.searchable_summary: record bioturbation intensity using Bioturbation Index

methods.description: Radiocarbon dating of plant matter and shells using Accelerator Mass Spectrometry (AMS) and calibration of ages.

steps.description: Accelerator Mass Spectrometry radiocarbon (AMS 14 C) dating was carried out at Beta Analytic Inc. on 27 samples of plant matter and shells.
steps.searchable_summary: perform AMS radiocarbon dating on samples

steps.description: The conventional ages were calibrated using the software Calib version 7.1.0 applying the curves IntCal13 for plant material samples and MARINE13 for shell samples.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate radiocarbon ages using Calib software

methods.description: OSL dating of quartz silt and sand samples to determine equivalent dose and final OSL ages.

steps.description: Samples were collected from the split section of borehole cores, wrapped with aluminium foil, and processed under controlled red light to determine the equivalent dose (De) and the final OSL ages.
steps.searchable_summary: process samples under red light to determine OSL ages

steps.description: Measurements were carried out with a TL-DA-20 Risø TL/OSL reader equipped with blue LEDs for stimulation and a 90 Sr/90 Y beta source for irradiation.
steps.searchable_summary: use TL/OSL reader for OSL measurements

steps.description: The environmental dose rate was determined considering contributions of radionuclides in sediments and cosmic rays and using the Dose Rate and Age Calculator software.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate environmental dose rate using software

methods.description: Calculation of age-depth models and accumulation rates using Bacon software.

steps.description: Age-depth models and accumulation rates were calculated using the Bacon age-depth modelling software, with depth data every 10 to 20 cm and ages calculated to the depth of the uppermost dating sample.
steps.searchable_summary: use Bacon software for age-depth modeling
